purpose: Validates this repo's country-level trade-deficit bulletin against TRADESTAT's own country x commodity data, and identifies exactly which HS chapters drive the four largest bilateral relationships (China, Russia, UAE, USA).
source: TRADESTAT (tradestat.commerce.gov.in/eidb/country_wise_all_commodities_export and .../country_wise_all_commodities_import), 2-digit HS level, US$ Million, FY2025-26

cross_validation_against_tia_portal.note: Two independent Commerce Ministry data sources agree to within rounding on all four countries -- the same cross-check this repo ran on aggregate trade-balance figures now holds at the country level.

key_findings.china_import_concentration: Electrical machinery (HS85, $46.4bn) + mechanical machinery (HS84, $29.5bn) = 58% of all India's imports from China -- industrial inputs/capital goods, not consumer goods.
key_findings.russia_import_concentration: Mineral fuels (HS27, $47.8bn) = 86% of all India's imports from Russia. Fertilisers (HS31, $3.1bn, +69.5% growth) is a fast-growing secondary link.
key_findings.uae_import_split: Gems & pearls (HS71, $24.8bn) and mineral fuels (HS27, $24.3bn) are almost exactly tied, together 77% of UAE imports -- a more even gold/petroleum split than a prior pass through this data implied.
key_findings.usa_export_concentration: Electrical machinery (HS85, $25.7bn, +61.5% growth) is India's largest export to the USA -- the same HS chapter that dominates China's imports into India, consistent with a component-import/finished-goods-export assembly pattern.
